jokes.text: The guy whose name you forbid me from saying out loud because it fills you with rage.
jokes.setup: Ben warns Leslie about someone being at the meeting
jokes.punchline: The dramatic description reveals Leslie's intense hatred
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven comedy that sets up Leslie's emotional state while being funny on its own

jokes.text: I'm perfectly civil. He's the stupid garbage head doodoo face.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims she can be perfectly civil
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves she can't with childish name-calling
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction showing Leslie's inability to be rational about Ron, with memorable childish insult

jokes.text: It's time to trade those dumb old trees for a buttload of cash.
jokes.setup: Formal legal announcement about land sale
jokes.punchline: Jessica's crude, dismissive phrasing in official quote
jokes.explanation: Jessica's shallow materialism expressed in hilariously blunt terms

jokes.text: I'm gonna work until I'm 100 and then cut back to four days a week. Oh, God. I'm already so bored thinking about that one day off. Maybe I'll go to law school or something.
jokes.setup: Leslie talks about retirement
jokes.punchline: Even theoretical retirement is too boring, needs backup plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope character moment - workaholic nature taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Hello, former strange person I used to friend.
jokes.setup: Leslie awkwardly greets Ron
jokes.punchline: Bizarre grammar making 'friend' a verb to avoid saying 'Ron'
jokes.explanation: Inventive wordplay that shows Leslie's awkwardness while avoiding Ron's name

jokes.text: You're looking very Ron-like.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to compliment Ron
jokes.punchline: Using his own name as descriptor while refusing to say it directly
jokes.explanation: Clever continuation of Leslie avoiding Ron's name while still referencing him

jokes.text: No, I don't! I have bangs now! I've never known what bangs are, and I don't intend to learn!
jokes.setup: Leslie comments on Ron's unchanged hair
jokes.punchline: Ron's aggressive denial about bangs he clearly doesn't have
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - stubborn masculinity about hair terminology

jokes.text: Very Good Building and Development Company. I wanted to convey the quality of our work without seeming flashy.
jokes.setup: Ron explains his company name
jokes.punchline: Hilariously literal, unimaginative name presented as thoughtful branding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron Swanson character comedy - his practical, anti-marketing approach taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Hell yeah, pimp! He's on Team Gryzzl now.
jokes.setup: Ron asked about company involvement
jokes.punchline: Tech bro's inappropriate enthusiasm and slang
jokes.explanation: Basic tech bro parody, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yes. The land has good sky.
jokes.setup: Describing the beautiful land features
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous assessment of sky as land feature
jokes.explanation: Absurd tech bro perspective on nature, economically delivered

jokes.text: Leslie! I couldn't find that file you wanted, but I did find a file called Bird Census 1980. And it's empty.
jokes.setup: Ed reports on file search
jokes.punchline: Useless discovery of meaningless empty file
jokes.explanation: Government bureaucracy incompetence joke, well-executed but familiar

jokes.text: Get out of here, Ed! I fired you. Right. If anybody wants to hang, I will be at Subway. He's very stupid.
jokes.setup: Leslie fires Ed
jokes.punchline: Ed's oblivious response and casual Subway invitation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ed's obliviousness to being fired

jokes.text: Babe, I just dropped off Leslie's triplets with her mom. All three of 'em. Nailed it.
jokes.setup: Andy reporting on babysitting duty
jokes.punchline: Being proud of dropping off the correct number of children
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - being proud of accomplishing the most basic requirement of childcare. The 'all three of 'em' and 'nailed it' sell his simple pride.

jokes.text: Welcome back to the Johnny Karate Super Awesome Musical Explosion Show.
jokes.setup: Andy hosts his children's show
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top show title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy Dwyer character moment - childish enthusiasm in professional setting

jokes.text: Andy, no. Ninjas attack! Andy. No. That's my crotch. Okay, guys. Three more kicks apiece.
jokes.setup: Andy's children's show with martial arts
jokes.punchline: Kids accidentally attacking Andy's crotch during demonstration
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Andy's incompetence at managing children's activities

jokes.text: Oh, Leslie, I have always liked you. Yeah, no, you haven't though.
jokes.setup: Jessica makes false claim about liking Leslie
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate, blunt correction
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan moment where Leslie cuts through Jessica's fake pleasantries

jokes.text: But I also like money. And money pays for my lifestyle. Leslie doesn't pay for my lifestyle. Money does. So, between money and Leslie... You see the bind that I'm in.
jokes.setup: Jessica explaining her decision process
jokes.punchline: Presenting obvious choice between money and Leslie as difficult dilemma
jokes.explanation: Jessica's shallow materialism presented as fake moral dilemma

jokes.text: Who is Tom Haverford? He's a mentor, a lover, a hero. But who is my hero? Simple. It's me five years from now.
jokes.setup: Tom asked for basic spelling information
jokes.punchline: Launches into grandiose self-description and philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford character moment - turning simple request into self-aggrandizing speech

jokes.text: You are looking at one of Indiana Business Monthly's 35 under 35. I own Pawnee's hottest restaurant, two fast casual eateries, and the Tommy Chopper. We serve chopped salads out of a decommissioned military helicopter.
jokes.setup: Tom listing his business accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Escalation to absurd helicopter restaurant concept
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from normal businesses to completely absurd helicopter restaurant

jokes.text: Wanna wake me up when you're done boring us to death, am I right, Janet? Write down that I'm funny.
jokes.setup: Tom criticizes Ben's speech preferences
jokes.punchline: Immediately instructs interviewer to record his supposed humor
jokes.explanation: Self-aware Tom moment where he tries to control his public image

jokes.text: Wait. You don't think this is all an elaborate setup to pelt me with things, do you?
jokes.setup: Ben talking about being honored at gala
jokes.punchline: Paranoid reference to his past humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ben's traumatic past, showing his ongoing insecurity

jokes.text: Andy, we're planning our whole week, like old people! We used to be spontaneous and weird. We used to eat cereal out of Frisbees because we didn't have any bowls.
jokes.setup: April realizes they've become domestic
jokes.punchline: Specific memory of eating cereal from Frisbees due to lack of bowls
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing April's horror at becoming normal

jokes.text: We used to eat cereal out of Frisbees because we didn't have any bowls. You once broke a rib trying to wrestle one of those inflatable floppy guys at a car dealership.
jokes.setup: April lamenting their lost spontaneity
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd examples of their past chaos
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between past chaotic behavior and current responsible planning. The inflatable guy wrestling is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: You once broke a rib trying to wrestle one of those inflatable floppy guys at a car dealership. I won.
jokes.setup: April reminiscing about Andy's past wildness
jokes.punchline: Andy's proud claim of victory despite serious injury
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy moment - turning serious injury into victory, perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: We're boring people who bore each other by being boring.
jokes.setup: April's existential crisis about becoming boring
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on their boringness through boring repetition
jokes.explanation: The repetition of 'boring' emphasizes April's despair about their transformation.

jokes.text: Well, I only have $2 million in the discretionary fund, and I've already used some of it to make Thomas Jefferson sexier in those Mount Rushmore promotions. Which, by the way, attendance has been up. That's not a coincidence.
jokes.setup: April explaining budget constraints
jokes.punchline: Absurd use of funds to sexualize historical monuments
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd government waste joke with April's deadpan confidence

jokes.text: My son sells them on Etsy. He is a huge disappointment.
jokes.setup: Ken mentions his son's bolo tie business
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ken Hotate deadpan moment - devastating parental disappointment delivered casually

jokes.text: So you would like the Wamapoke people to pay the federal government millions of dollars to buy land that was stolen from us by the federal government? And we don't even get to keep the land?
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Ken for donation to buy land for park
jokes.punchline: Ken points out the absurd irony of the situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary disguised as character comedy, highlighting historical injustice with perfect logic

jokes.text: I'm so crazy and spontaneous, I don't even need this sling anymore. Yes. Oh. Did you hear that pop?
jokes.setup: Andy tries to prove he's still wild by removing sling
jokes.punchline: Immediate injury from his 'spontaneous' act
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy with immediate consequences for Andy's recklessness

jokes.text: You ruined it. We have to figure something else out. Should I still take the Zantac? Yeah, you always feel better when you do.
jokes.setup: April frustrated that Andy ruined their spontaneous moment
jokes.punchline: They continue being responsible even while complaining about it
jokes.explanation: The cycle continues - they hate being responsible but can't help themselves.

jokes.text: I'm gonna take a Zantac. All that salt will give me heartburn. Oh, God. You ruined it. We have to figure something else out. Should I still take the Zantac? Yeah, you always feel better when you do.
jokes.setup: Andy about to eat jar of olives as spontaneous act
jokes.punchline: Responsible health precaution ruins the rebellious moment
jokes.explanation: Great moment showing how their responsible adult habits sabotage attempts at spontaneity

jokes.text: So I'm kind of totally on Ron's side.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to rally her old team
jokes.punchline: Tom's casual betrayal for business reasons
jokes.explanation: Tom's casual betrayal fits his opportunistic character perfectly.

jokes.text: Shia LaBeouf designed wedding dresses do not come cheap.
jokes.setup: Donna explaining why she needs money for her wedding
jokes.punchline: Bizarre celebrity designer reference for wedding dresses
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity reference that's unexpected and memorable

jokes.text: Yes, I go by Terry now. Because there was a guy at National Parks. He was already named Larry, so they suggested I go by Terry. And then I said my real name is Gary, and they said, 'Who cares?'
jokes.setup: Terry explains his new name
jokes.punchline: Colleagues' complete indifference to his actual identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of Jerry's running gag with new workplace showing same disrespect

jokes.text: Unless you're talking about a map of the cities with the most obese pets.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about Pawnee barely being on the map
jokes.punchline: Specific, embarrassing statistic about pet obesity
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Pawnee's quirky problems

jokes.text: And that man was me. Tom Haverford, owner and proprietor of the effortlessly chic, celebrity-packed Italian restaurant, Tom's Bistro.
jokes.setup: Tom building up to introduce 'one great man'
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's talking about himself and promoting his restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom move - using someone else's introduction as an opportunity for self-promotion.

jokes.text: I simply took a skewer for one shrimp and added many shrimp to it. They're long enough for five or six. I fit seven on this one. It's a masterpiece.
jokes.setup: Leslie accuses Ron of stealing
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact explanation of his shrimp efficiency innovation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment treating simple food hack as major achievement

jokes.text: And I say, 'Not for me. I'm pretty amazing at being humble.'
jokes.setup: Tom talks about staying humble despite success
jokes.punchline: Completely contradicts humility by bragging about being humble
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-defeating logic that captures Tom's oblivious narcissism

jokes.text: And I say, 'Not for me. I'm pretty amazing at being humble.'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining how people ask if it's hard to stay humble
jokes.punchline: His completely un-humble response about being amazing at humility
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction - claiming to be amazing at being humble is the opposite of humble, delivered with zero self-awareness.

jokes.text: And thank you to Tom for that moving tribute to you.
jokes.setup: Ben acknowledging Tom's introduction
jokes.punchline: Dry observation that Tom's speech was entirely about himself
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical callback to Tom's self-centered introduction

jokes.text: How about this? We shut off the lights, turn 'em back on. Everybody's fancy jewelry is missing. Meanwhile, you and I are already on a boat to the airport.
jokes.setup: April trying to think of spontaneous things to do
jokes.punchline: Proposing an elaborate heist with impossible logistics
jokes.explanation: April's criminal mind jumping immediately to elaborate theft, with the illogical 'boat to airport' detail.

jokes.text: How about this? We shut off the lights, turn 'em back on. Everybody's fancy jewelry is missing. Meanwhile, you and I are already on a boat to the airport.
jokes.setup: April suggesting spontaneous activity
jokes.punchline: Elaborate heist plan with illogical escape route
jokes.explanation: Good April character moment with absurd criminal scheme and nonsensical logistics

jokes.text: Being responsible adults sucks butts.
jokes.setup: April realizing she got rid of stink bombs before babysitting
jokes.punchline: Her childish language expressing adult frustration
jokes.explanation: April's frustration expressed in deliberately childish language to contrast with her unwanted maturity.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get naked. I'm gonna get up there. Everyone is gonna see my wiener. I mean, you've seen it. You know how dumb it looks.
jokes.setup: Andy planning to streak across stage
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating description of his anatomy
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy oversharing with self-deprecating twist

jokes.text: It's like 'Voldemort' or 'Ron.'
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining Morningstar is a forbidden word
jokes.punchline: Comparing Ron's name to Voldemort as equally unspeakable
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that perfectly captures Leslie's dramatic feelings about Ron

jokes.text: They're still gonna serve that cake, right?
jokes.setup: Chaos and cake destruction at the gala
jokes.punchline: Andy's only concern is whether he'll still get cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy priorities - food concerns override dramatic confrontation

jokes.text: I love you, too. I love your skin. Give me your skin.
jokes.setup: Jessica interacting with new tablet
jokes.punchline: AI's disturbing escalation from love to creepy demands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from normal AI to horror movie territory, very quotable

jokes.text: It's the creepy warehouse district. It's mostly just raccoons and old car batteries.
jokes.setup: April describing unfamiliar part of Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific and depressing neighborhood description
jokes.explanation: Good specific detail that paints vivid picture of urban decay

jokes.text: Truth is they didn't ask me to introduce you. I asked them if I could do it.
jokes.setup: Tom apologizing for his self-centered introduction
jokes.punchline: Reveals he volunteered for the opportunity to talk about himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's shameless self-promotion

jokes.text: Is this a circle? Or is it an O? Is Oprah involved in your bid?
jokes.setup: Leslie presents zero dollar bid
jokes.punchline: Jessica's confusion leading to Oprah theory
jokes.explanation: Jessica's shallow pop culture associations and inability to recognize zero

jokes.text: Well, yes, admittedly, it did make the fish taste delicious, but it was a PR nightmare.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions hot fudge pipeline disaster
jokes.punchline: Jessica's misplaced priorities about environmental disaster
jokes.explanation: Jessica's twisted perspective on environmental damage as PR issue

jokes.text: It says here there are 12 closets, three bomb shelters, five dumbwaiters, two and three-eighths baths, no kitchens. It's a fairly standard layout.
jokes.setup: Realtor describing house features
jokes.punchline: Presenting completely bizarre layout as normal
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with deadpan delivery treating insane house as normal

jokes.text: No. This was a holding cell for people who went insane on the assembly line.
jokes.setup: Andy asks about doll head factory
jokes.punchline: Much darker revelation about the building's true purpose
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation from quirky factory to mental health facility

jokes.text: After 47 years living here, I decided to move to Orlando to be closer to Disney World.
jokes.setup: Agent explaining why they're selling
jokes.punchline: Senior citizen's Disney World obsession as life motivation
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about adult Disney obsession

jokes.text: Fwar, Dips, Winshares, Gritt, Babip, Pecota, Vorp and Eckstein
jokes.setup: Lawyer introducing his firm
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous law firm name made of baseball statistics
jokes.explanation: Clever baseball statistics reference disguised as law firm, appeals to specific audience

jokes.text: You know, I still think Kevin James was a weird choice for the reboot. Oh, I don't. I think he nailed it.
jokes.setup: April references Jason Bourne quote
jokes.punchline: Reveals Kevin James played Jason Bourne in their universe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist world-building joke about impossible casting choice

jokes.text: And also I made cookies, but Ron is not allowed to eat them and they say, 'Prepare for War' on them, but the O in the word 'for' is a heart.
jokes.setup: Leslie declaring war on her friends
jokes.punchline: Aggressive war cookies with heart detail showing her conflicted nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - trying to be aggressive but can't help being caring and detail-oriented

jokes.text: but the O in the word 'for' is a heart. And I ate a lot of 'em on the way over here.
jokes.setup: Leslie's war cookies with aggressive message
jokes.punchline: Cute detail followed by admission she ate the ammunition
jokes.explanation: Leslie's inability to be truly aggressive even in war mode, plus her typical lack of self-control with baked goods.
